highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.111: Opus 4.7 xhigh Effort Level & Auto Mode for Max
highlights.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 now supports a new 'xhigh' effort level, sitting between 'high' and 'max', giving you finer control over the speed vs. intelligence tradeoff. You can tune this via the /effort command, --effort flag, or model settings. Max subscribers also gain access to auto mode when using Opus 4.7.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.113: Native Binary & Domain Blocking for Sandbox
highlights.summary: Claude Code now spawns a native binary per platform instead of bundled JavaScript, which should improve startup performance and reliability. A new sandbox.network.deniedDomains setting lets you block specific domains even when a broader allowedDomains wildcard would otherwise permit them, giving you more precise network security control.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.110: New /tui Fullscreen Mode & /focus Command
highlights.summary: A new /tui fullscreen command switches Claude Code to flicker-free rendering within your current conversation, improving readability during long sessions. The Ctrl+O shortcut now exclusively toggles verbose transcript view, while a new /focus command handles the focus view toggle that was previously combined.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has officially announced Claude Opus 4.7, their latest and most capable model now available in Claude Code. This is the model powering the new xhigh effort level and auto mode features, making it a significant upgrade for developers tackling complex coding tasks.

highlights.title: FULL Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners in 2026! (Step-By-Step)
highlights.summary: This step-by-step YouTube tutorial is designed for developers who are brand new to Claude Code in 2026. It walks through setup, basic commands, and practical use cases to get you productive quickly inside VS Code.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial promises to get you from zero to productive with Claude Code in just 30 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want a fast, focused overview. It covers key workflows and commands that will have an immediate impact on your daily coding sessions.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Down for >12 Hours (HN Discussion)
highlights.summary: A Hacker News thread highlights a widespread issue where Claude Code OAuth login was broken for over 12 hours, leaving users stuck at the 'Paste code here if prompted' step with no way to authenticate. If you experienced login issues recently, this thread confirms it was a known outage — check claudestatus.com for current service health before debugging your own setup.

highlights.title: Claude Code Routines Documentation
highlights.summary: Anthropic's official documentation on Claude Code Routines explains how to define repeatable, automated workflows that Claude can execute on a schedule or on demand. This is a powerful feature for developers who want to automate repetitive tasks like running tests, formatting code, or syncing documentation without manual prompting.

tips_of_the_day: Use /effort xhigh with Opus 4.7 when tackling complex refactors or architecture tasks — it sits between 'high' and 'max' intelligence, giving you a better balance of speed and depth without burning through your quota as fast as 'max'.

Add sandbox.network.deniedDomains to your Claude Code settings to blocklist specific third-party domains (like telemetry endpoints) even if you've already set a broad allowedDomains wildcard — this gives you surgical control over what your agent can reach.

Before assuming a Claude Code bug is local to your machine, always check claudestatus.com first. Authentication and API issues are sometimes platform-wide outages, and knowing that within seconds can save you hours of fruitless troubleshooting.
